title: [phase:critique] [family:rest-signup] [provider_hint:codex] For Supabase Auth, if email signup is enabled and confirmations are off, what exact REST endpoint and payload yields an access_token using the anon key? Answer in 2 bullets only.
summary_text: - `POST https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1/signup` with headers `apikey: <anon-key>` and `Content-Type: application/json` (optionally also `Authorization: Bearer <anon-key>`). With Confirm email disabled, Supabase says signup returns both `user` and `session`, and the Auth API’s signup route is `/signup`: https://supabase.com/docs/reference/javascript/auth-signup , https://github.com/supabase/auth - Payload: `{"email":"user@example.com","password":"your-password"}` (`data` is optional). On this path, when confirmations are off, the response includes a session containing `access_token` and `refresh_token`: https://supabase.com/docs/reference/javascript/auth-signup , https://github.com/supabase/auth
